message:
✨ Drag-and-drop multi-cluster deploy with inter-card messaging (#176)

* Wire cluster_groups and missions cards into Deploy dashboard

Add cross-card DnD support to the Deploy page so workloads can be
dragged onto cluster groups to trigger multi-cluster deployment.
Also adds the new cards to the Deploy dashboard defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Add dynamic cluster group query evaluation and AI query endpoints

Extend ClusterGroup struct with Kind (static/dynamic), Query, and
LastEvaluated fields. Add POST /api/cluster-groups/evaluate endpoint
that filters clusters by label selectors and resource conditions
(AND logic). Add POST /api/cluster-groups/ai-query endpoint that
uses the AI provider to convert natural language into structured
cluster group queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* Extend useClusterGroups hook with dynamic group types and methods

Add ClusterGroupKind, ClusterFilter, ClusterGroupQuery types.
Add evaluateGroup(), previewQuery(), and generateAIQuery() methods.
Migrate existing groups without kind field to 'static'.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add static/dynamic toggle, query builder, and AI assistant to Cluster Groups card

- Static mode: existing manual cluster picker (unchanged behavior)
- Dynamic mode with two creation tabs:
  - Query Builder: label selector input + filter chip editor for resource
    conditions (healthy, reachable, cpuCores, memoryGB, nodeCount, podCount)
  - AI Assistant: natural language input generates structured queries
    with suggested group names
- Preview Matches button evaluates queries before saving
- Dynamic groups show refresh button and last-evaluated timestamp
- Visual indicators (Zap badge) distinguish dynamic from static groups
- Edit form supports switching between static and dynamic modes
- Backward compatible: existing groups without kind default to static

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add GPU filters, compact cluster badges, and rename missions card

Backend:
- Add gpuCount and gpuType filter support to evaluate endpoint
- Fetch node data when GPU filters are present (not just for label selectors)
- Add compareStringSet helper for case-insensitive substring matching
- Add clusterGPUCount/clusterGPUTypes aggregation helpers

Frontend:
- Add GPU Count (number) and GPU Type (text) to filter fields
- Handle text filter type with contains/excludes operators
- Add compact cluster health dots on collapsed group rows (max 4 inline)
- Rename missions card to deployment_missions to separate from AI missions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix auth headers on cluster group API calls and add GPU to AI prompt

- Add Bearer token from localStorage to all cluster-groups fetch calls
- Previously evaluate and AI query endpoints returned 401 because the
  hook used plain fetch without Authorization headers
- Update AI system prompt to include gpuCount and gpuType filter fields
- Add GPU info context for AI query generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Add dependency resolution, pre-deploy confirmation dialog, and Resource Marshall card

Cross-cluster deployments now resolve and copy all dependent resources
(ConfigMaps, Secrets, ServiceAccounts, RBAC, PVCs, Services, Ingresses,
NetworkPolicies, HPAs, PDBs) alongside the workload. A pre-deploy
confirmation dialog previews the dependency tree before deploying, and
a new Resource Marshall dashboard card lets users explore workload
dependencies interactively via cascading Cluster → Namespace → Workload
dropdowns.

Backend:
- Add dependency walker (pkg/k8s/dependencies.go) with transitive resolution
- Integrate dependency resolution into DeployWorkload with skip-if-exists logic
- Add GET /api/workloads/resolve-deps/:cluster/:namespace/:name dry-run endpoint
- Add DeployedDep type and Dependencies/Warnings fields to DeployResponse

Frontend:
- Add useDependencies.ts shared hook for dependency resolution
- Add DeployConfirmDialog with grouped dependency preview
- Wire confirmation dialog into Deploy.tsx and Dashboard.tsx
- Add Resource Marshall card with dependency tree explorer
- Register card in cardRegistry, AddCardModal, templates, formatCardTitle
- Update Missions card to show dependency summary post-deploy
- Update cardEvents with deploy:result event type

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+    { type: 'workload_deployment', title: 'Workloads', description: 'Multi-cluster workload deployment with status and scaling', visualization: 'table' },

-    { type: 'missions', title: 'Missions', description: 'Track deployment missions with per-cluster rollout progress', visualization: 'status' },
+    { type: 'deployment_missions', title: 'Deployment Missions', description: 'Track deployment missions with per-cluster rollout progress', visualization: 'status' },
+    { type: 'resource_marshall', title: 'Resource Marshall', description: 'Explore workload dependency trees — ConfigMaps, Secrets, RBAC, Services, and more', visualization: 'table' },

@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'
+
+export interface ResolvedDependency {
+  kind: string
+  name: string
+  namespace: string
+  optional: boolean
+  order: number
+}
+
+export interface DependencyResolution {
+  workload: string
+  kind: string
+  namespace: string
+  cluster: string
+  dependencies: ResolvedDependency[]
+  warnings: string[]
+}
+
+function authHeaders(): Record<string, string> {
+  const token = localStorage.getItem('token')
+  return token ? { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } : {}
+}
+
+/**
+ * Hook to resolve dependencies for a workload (dry-run).
+ * Used by the pre-deploy confirmation dialog and the Resource Marshall card.
+ */
+export function useResolveDependencies() {
+  const [data, setData] = useState<DependencyResolution | null>(null)
+  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false)
+  const [error, setError] = useState<Error | null>(null)
+
+  const resolve = useCallback(async (
+    cluster: string,
+    namespace: string,
+    name: string,
+  ): Promise<DependencyResolution | null> => {
+    setIsLoading(true)
+    setError(null)
+    setData(null)
+
+    try {
+      const res = await fetch(
+        `/api/workloads/resolve-deps/${encodeURIComponent(cluster)}/${encodeURIComponent(namespace)}/${encodeURIComponent(name)}`,
+        { headers: authHeaders() },
+      )
+      if (!res.ok) {
+        const errorData = await res.json().catch(() => ({ error: res.statusText }))
+        throw new Error(errorData.error || `Failed to resolve dependencies: ${res.statusText}`)
+      }
+      const result: DependencyResolution = await res.json()
+      setData(result)
+      return result
+    } catch (err) {
+      const error = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error('Unknown error')
+      setError(error)
+      return null
+    } finally {
+      setIsLoading(false)
+    }
+  }, [])
+
+  const reset = useCallback(() => {
+    setData(null)
+    setError(null)
+    setIsLoading(false)
+  }, [])
+
+  return { data, isLoading, error, resolve, reset }
+}
